name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram is a money transfer and payment services company with 24+ locations across Louisville, operating through retail partners like Walmart, CVS, and independent markets.
description_long:
MoneyGram is a global money transfer and financial services company that operates by partnering with retail locations rather than maintaining standalone storefronts. In Louisville, Kentucky, the company maintains a network of 24 service locations embedded within existing retailers including major chains like Walmart and CVS, as well as independent markets and delis. This partnership model allows MoneyGram to distribute its services across diverse neighborhoods and commercial areas throughout the city.

MoneyGram's core offerings center on money transfer services—both domestic and international—along with related financial services typical of the check-cashing and money services category. The company enables customers to send and receive money, process money orders, conduct wire transfers, and handle bill payments. Services are available both online and offline, providing flexibility for customers who prefer digital transactions versus in-person visits. The Louisville location page emphasizes ease of access and convenience through their multi-location network.

The company distinguishes itself through its extensive retail partnership network, which provides geographic accessibility that standalone check-cashing shops cannot match. Operating through established retailers like Walmart and CVS means extended hours (many locations open until 9-10 PM), established store infrastructure, and locations in both residential and commercial areas. This distribution strategy targets customers who may already be shopping at these retailers and need quick money transfer or payment services without dedicated trips.

MoneyGram serves a practical financial function for customers needing money transfer, bill payment, and money order services, but operates in a competitive space with other money transfer providers and banks offering similar services. The company's reliance on retail partners means service quality and availability depend on individual franchise locations. For customers seeking check-cashing, money orders, or wire transfers, MoneyGram provides convenient access through familiar retail environments, though pricing and fees are not detailed on the location search page.
pros: 24 locations throughout Louisville providing geographic coverage across the city
Extended operating hours at many locations (open until 9-10 PM, some until midnight)
Embedded in major retailers (Walmart, CVS) for convenience during regular shopping
Both online and offline transaction options available
Partner locations include independent markets serving diverse neighborhoods
Easy online location finder with address, city, state, and ZIP code search functionality
Established brand with multiple contact methods listed for each location
cons: Pricing and fee structures not disclosed on the location page
Service quality and availability dependent on individual franchise/partner locations
No standalone MoneyGram offices—reliant on retail partners for access
Limited service information provided on the location page regarding specific offerings and rates
No transparency about international transfer rates or sending limitations
best_for: Customers needing quick money transfers who already shop at Walmart or CVS
People requiring bill payment or money order services with flexible evening hours
Individuals seeking convenient location access without dedicated trips to specialty check-cashing shops
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for customers needing money transfer, bill payment, or money order services who value convenience through established retail locations with extended hours. The main caveat is that fees, rates, and specific service terms are not disclosed on their location pages, and service quality depends on individual partner locations, so customers should verify specific offerings and pricing before visiting.
services: Location-based service access through retail partners
